texto: We do not know if she collapsed because of overwhelming joy, extreme surprise, grave disappointment, or heavy anxiety that for the next months and years she would live with a human male, because in fact she had been honest when she told her girlfriends that she had given up on men, OR NONE OF THE ABOVE.
explicacao: Citação de Kyoko Yoshida sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “We do not know if she collapsed because of overwhelming joy, extreme surprise, grave disappointme…” articula amizade, anthology, asian-literature em registro de amizade, com relevância para contextos como termino e asian-literature.

frase_original: We do not know if she collapsed because of overwhelming joy, extreme surprise, grave disappointment, or heavy anxiety that for the next months and years she would live with a human male, because in fact she had been honest when she told her girlfriends that she had given up on men, OR NONE OF THE ABOVE.

texto: If rewriting equals rereading, we must logically conclude that writing is reading. If this is indeed the case, how could we possibly write under a ban on reading? The only way left is mouth-to-mouth – poets and storytellers recite their pieces and before we can commit them to memory, everything vanishes into thin air.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Kyoko Yoshida que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “If rewriting equals rereading, we must logically conclude that writing is reading. If this is ind…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, authors, ban, com foco em ban e books.

frase_original: If rewriting equals rereading, we must logically conclude that writing is reading. If this is indeed the case, how could we possibly write under a ban on reading? The only way left is mouth-to-mouth – poets and storytellers recite their pieces and before we can commit them to memory, everything vanishes into thin air.

texto: No, I am not imagining a book-burning, warmongering, anti-intellectual fascist regime – in my plan, there is no place for re ghters who light up the Homers and Lady Murasakis and Cao Xueqins stashed under your bed – because, for starters, I’m not banning literature per se. I’m banning the reading of literature. Purchasing and collecting books and other forms of literature remains perfectly legitimate as long as you don’t peruse the literature at hand.
